segments.text: Jacob also went on his way, and the angels of God met him.
When Jacob saw them, he said,
This is the camp of God.
segments.speaker: Jacob

segments.text: So he named that place Mahanaim.
Jacob sent messengers ahead of him to his brother Esau in the land of Seir, the country of Edom.
He instructed them,
You are to say to my master Esau, ‘Your servant Jacob says: I have been staying with Laban and have remained there until now.
segments.speaker: Jacob

segments.text: I have oxen, donkeys, flocks, menservants, and maidservants. I have sent this message to inform my master, so that I may find favor in your sight.’
segments.speaker: Jacob

segments.text: When the messengers returned to Jacob, they said,
We went to your brother Esau, and now he is coming to meet you—he and four hundred men with him.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: In great fear and distress, Jacob divided his people into two camps, as well as the flocks and herds and camels.
He thought,
If Esau comes and attacks one camp, then the other camp can escape.
segments.speaker: Jacob

segments.text: Then Jacob declared,
O God of my father Abraham, God of my father Isaac, the LORD who told me, ‘Go back to your country and to your kindred, and I will make you prosper,’
segments.speaker: Jacob

segments.text: I am unworthy of all the kindness and faithfulness You have shown Your servant. Indeed, with only my staff I came across the Jordan, but now I have become two camps.
segments.speaker: Jacob

segments.text: Please deliver me from the hand of my brother Esau, for I am afraid that he may come and attack me and the mothers and children with me.
segments.speaker: Jacob

segments.text: But You have said, ‘I will surely make you prosper, and I will make your offspring like the sand of the sea, too numerous to count.’
segments.speaker: Jacob

segments.text: Jacob spent the night there, and from what he had brought with him, he selected a gift for his brother Esau:
200 female goats, 20 male goats, 200 ewes, 20 rams,
30 milk camels with their young, 40 cows, 10 bulls, 20 female donkeys, and 10 male donkeys.
He entrusted them to his servants in separate herds and told them,
Go on ahead of me, and keep some distance between the herds.
segments.speaker: Jacob

segments.text: He instructed the one in the lead,
When my brother Esau meets you and asks, ‘To whom do you belong, where are you going, and whose animals are these before you?’
segments.speaker: Jacob

segments.text: then you are to say, ‘They belong to your servant Jacob. They are a gift, sent to my lord Esau. And behold, Jacob is behind us.’
segments.speaker: Jacob

segments.text: He also instructed the second, the third, and all those following behind the herds:
When you meet Esau, you are to say the same thing to him.
segments.speaker: Jacob

segments.text: You are also to say, ‘Look, your servant Jacob is right behind us.’
segments.speaker: Jacob

segments.text: For he thought,
I will appease Esau with the gift that is going before me. After that I can face him, and perhaps he will accept me.
segments.speaker: Jacob

segments.text: So Jacob’s gifts went on before him, while he spent the night in the camp.
During the night Jacob got up and took his two wives, his two maidservants, and his eleven sons, and crossed the ford of the Jabbok.
He took them and sent them across the stream, along with all his possessions.
So Jacob was left all alone, and there a man wrestled with him until daybreak.
When the man saw that he could not overpower Jacob, he struck the socket of Jacob’s hip and dislocated it as they wrestled.
Then the man said,
Let me go, for it is daybreak.
segments.speaker: The man

segments.text: But Jacob replied,
I will not let you go unless you bless me.
segments.speaker: Jacob

segments.text: What is your name?
segments.speaker: The man

segments.text: the man asked.
Jacob,
segments.speaker: The man

segments.text: he replied.
Then the man said,
Your name will no longer be Jacob, but Israel, because you have struggled with God and with men, and you have prevailed.
segments.speaker: The man

segments.text: And Jacob requested,
Please tell me your name.
segments.speaker: Jacob

segments.text: But he replied,
Why do you ask my name?
segments.speaker: The man

segments.text: Then he blessed Jacob there.
So Jacob named the place Peniel, saying,
Indeed, I have seen God face to face, and yet my life was spared.
segments.speaker: Jacob

segments.text: The sun rose above him as he passed by Penuel, and he was limping because of his hip.
Therefore to this day the Israelites do not eat the tendon which is at the socket of the hip, because the socket of Jacob’s hip was struck near that tendon.
